parts: tocar la guitarra todas las noches.
rationale: era → imperfecto: describes a continuous past state ("when I was young" sets the habitual backdrop).
solía → imperfecto: soler + infinitive expresses habitual past action ("used to play every night").
mostró → pretérito: a single completed action on a specific day.
dijo → pretérito: a specific statement made at that moment, fully completed.

parts: una grieta pequeña en el cuerpo.
rationale: era → imperfecto: describes a characteristic/physical state of the guitar, not a change.
tenía → imperfecto: describes an ongoing physical feature — background description.
contó → pretérito: the act of telling is a discrete, completed event.
compró → pretérito: a specific purchase completed at one point in the past.
estaba → imperfecto: describes an emotional state acting as background context.
toqué → pretérito: playing those notes was a single completed action.
escuchaba → imperfecto: listening was ongoing/simultaneous background to my playing.
practicaba → imperfecto: practicing was also continuous; both actions overlap in duration.
se levantó → pretérito: de repente signals a sudden punctual interruption — a completed action.
dio → pretérito: a single completed gesture at that moment.

parts: Yo sabía que la música nos
rationale: unía → imperfecto: expresses a general ongoing truth or state in the past, not a specific event.
tocamos → pretérito: that specific night is a bounded, completed session.
nos dormimos → pretérito: falling asleep was a puntual completed event at the end of that night.

parts: esa guitarra, siempre pienso en él.
rationale: toco → presente: the frame shifts to today — "whenever I play" — contrasting past memory with present reality.
